title: VBr >10 kV E-Beam/Sputtered Vertical NiOx/(011) β-Ga2O3 HJDs with PFOM >2.3 GW/cm2
authors: Yizheng Liu, Carl Peterson, Chinmoy Nath Saha, Marko J. Tadjer, Sriram Krishnamoorthy
abstract: Beta-gallium oxide (β-Ga2O3) holds enormous potential for medium voltage range power electronic applications. This work reports VBr > 10 kV/Ron,sp = 43 mΩ*cm2 class edge terminated vertical heterojunction diodes (HJDs) with e-beam/sputtered nickel oxide (NiOx) stack on epitaxial (011) β-Ga2O3. The power figure of merit (PFOM) of the HJD exceeds 2.3 GW/cm2. The extracted parallel plane breakdown field is > 5.3 MV/cm, which is the highest reported electric field for thick (011) β-Ga2O3 epitaxial drift layer.
summary: This paper reports the development of high-performance beta-gallium oxide power diodes capable of withstanding voltages over 10 kV. By using a nickel oxide heterojunction to bypass the need for p-type doping, the researchers achieved a high power figure of merit, making this material a strong candidate for next-generation power grids and electric vehicle technology.
main_problem: The lack of reliable p-type doping in beta-gallium oxide (beta-Ga2O3) prevents the creation of high-voltage power electronic devices for applications like EV charging and AI data centers.
main_result: Demonstration of vertical heterojunction diodes (HJDs) with a breakdown voltage exceeding 10 kV and a power figure of merit (PFOM) greater than 2.3 GW/cm2.
method: Fabrication of NiOx/beta-Ga2O3 heterostructures using e-beam evaporation and reactive sputtering, combined with BCl3 ICP dry-etching and SiO2 field-plate formation.
model_or_system: Vertical heterojunction diodes (HJDs) featuring an e-beam/sputtered NiOx stack on an epitaxial (011) beta-Ga2O3 drift layer.
key_observables: Breakdown voltage (VBr), specific on-resistance (Ron,sp), Power Figure of Merit (PFOM), parallel-plane breakdown field, and turn-on voltage (Von).
important_parameters: Breakdown voltage > 10 kV, specific on-resistance = 43 mOhm*cm2, breakdown field > 5.3 MV/cm, and carrier density ~ 2 x 10^15 cm^-3.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the motivation for high-voltage power electronics, describes the fabrication process of the NiOx/beta-Ga2O3 heterostructure, presents the electrical characterization of the resulting HJDs, and concludes with the performance metrics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig.1. Schematics cross-section of (a) reference Schottky barrier diodes (SBDs) and (b) mesa-isolated/field-plated heterojunction diodes (MI/FP HJDs).
Fig.3. (a) C-V characteristics of MI/FP HJD on 1-mm dia. dimeison at 1 MHz with 1/C2 vs. voltage inset. (b) Doping vs. depth profile MI/FP HJD on 1-mm dia. dimeison at 1 MHz.

Fig.4. (a) Reverse breakdown characteristics of MI/FP HJDs for 60-μm dia. devices. (b) Reverse breakdown characteristics of MI/FP HJDs for 100-μm dia. devices. (c) J-V characteristics of 60-μm dia. device before and after 10 kV reverse bias in both linear and semi-log scale.

Fig.2. (a) Linear forward J-V characteristics of SBDs and MI/FP HJDs. (b) Semi-log scale J-V characteristics of SBDs and MI/FP HJDs. (c) Ron,sp vs. diode dimension for SBDs and MI/FP HJDs. (d) Pulsed I-V characteristics for SBD and MI/FP 1-mm dia. devices.

Fig. 5. benchmarks the Ron,sp vs. breakdown voltage (VBr) of heterojunction diodes fabricated in this work in comparison to various HJDs reported in the literatures. It is shown that the PFOM of the MI/FP HJDs begins exceeding the material limit of 4H-SiC at 10 kV reverse bias.
